name: Between Cintegabelle and Nailloux
region_intro: Road cycling around Auterive features routes through the rolling countryside of the Haute-Garonne department, characterized by agricultural landscapes and river valleys such as the La Lèze. The terrain primarily consists of paved rural roads connecting small towns and villages, offering varied gradients from flat sections to moderate climbs. This region provides a network of routes suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Auterive The most popular road cycling route is Between Cintegabelle and Nailloux, a 22.7 miles (36.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two local towns via rural roads. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Saint-Lys Church – Road along La Lèze loop from Auterive, a moderate 36.7 miles (59.1 km) path. This route follows the La Lèze river valley, offering views of the surrounding agricultural land. Local road cyclists also love the Saverdun – Cintegabelle loop from Auterive, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through the open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Road cycling around Auterive is defined by rolling countryside, river valleys, and rural roads, with a network offering options for easy road bike routes Auterive as well as more challenging long distance road bike trails Auterive. The routes in Auterive are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Auterive's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Auterive?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes available around Auterive, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Auterive?
A: Road cycling in Auterive features routes through the rolling countryside of the Haute-Garonne department. The terrain primarily consists of paved rural roads connecting small towns and villages, offering varied gradients from flat sections to moderate climbs.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Auterive?
A: Yes, Auterive offers several easy road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5351048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saverdun – Cintegabelle loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.7 miles (47.8 km) and leads through open countryside. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39191374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Labarthe-sur-Lèze – Saint-Lys Church loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find longer distance road cycling routes in the Auterive area?
A: For longer distances, consider routes that explore the river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26662899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Lys Church – Road along La Lèze loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.7 miles (59.1 km) path that follows the La Lèze river valley, offering extensive views of the agricultural land.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: While the region is characterized by rolling countryside, some routes offer more significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937071517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Cintegabelle and Nailloux&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, covers 22.7 miles (36.5 km) with over 550 meters of elevation gain, providing a good challenge.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the agricultural landscapes and river valleys. You can also explore nearby attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3091341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Notre-Dame des Bois Chapel&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin de Montbrun-Lauragais&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3571293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lèze River&lt;/a&gt; itself is a prominent natural feature.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Auterive?
A: Yes, many routes in the Auterive area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26662899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Lys Church – Road along La Lèze loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5351048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saverdun – Cintegabelle loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Auterive?
A: The road cycling routes in Auterive are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain and the peaceful rural roads connecting small towns.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Auterive?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Auterive, with mild temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically mild but may have more rainfall.

Q: Are there routes that pass through towns with cafes or amenities?
A: Many routes connect small towns and villages in the region, such as Cintegabelle, Nailloux, and Saverdun. These towns typically offer local amenities, including cafes and bakeries, where you can stop for refreshments.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that are less crowded or considered 'hidden gems'?
A: Given the extensive network of rural roads, many routes offer a sense of tranquility away from major traffic. Exploring the smaller loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38530104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auterive Town Hall loop from Les Baccarets d’En Bas&lt;/a&gt; can lead you to quieter, less-trafficked paths through the countryside.
